title: Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake

seo.title: Authentic Haemul Pajeon Recipe | Korean Seafood Pancake
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make the best Haemul Pajeon (Korean Seafood Pancake) at home with our easy, authentic recipe. Perfect for a delicious meal!
seo.h1: Authentic Haemul Pajeon Recipe: Korean Seafood Pancake
h2Headings: What is Haemul Pajeon (Korean Seafood Pancake)?
Ingredients You'll Need for Haemul Pajeon
Step-by-Step Instructions to Make Haemul Pajeon
Pro Tips for the Best Crispy Haemul Pajeon
Serving Suggestions for Your Seafood Pancake
Variations and Substitutions for Haemul Pajeon
seo.imageAlt: A freshly made Haemul Pajeon, a crispy Korean seafood pancake, garnished with green onions and dipping sauce.
seo.imageCaption: Crispy, savory, and packed with fresh seafood – our Haemul Pajeon recipe brings authentic Korean flavors to your kitchen.

description: Haemul Pajeon is a beloved Korean savory pancake, crispy on the outside and tender within, generously studded with a medley of fresh seafood and vibrant green onions. This delectable dish offers a delightful textural contrast and a burst of umami flavors, perfect as an appetizer or a light meal. Its golden-brown crust and flavorful filling make it an irresistible comfort food.

instructions.title: Prepare the Batter
instructions.text: In a large bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, rice flour (if using), salt, and cold water until smooth. Ensure there are no lumps. Whisk in the egg until fully incorporated. The batter should be slightly thinner than pancake batter.

instructions.title: Prepare Seafood and Vegetables
instructions.text: Thoroughly rinse and pat dry your mixed seafood. Cut larger pieces of shrimp or squid into bite-sized pieces. Prepare the green onions by cutting them into approximately 3-inch lengths. Thinly slice the yellow onion and red chili (if using).

instructions.title: Make the Dipping Sauce
instructions.text: In a small bowl, combine soy sauce, rice vinegar, sugar, sesame oil, and toasted sesame seeds. Stir until the sugar dissolves. Set aside for serving.

instructions.title: Combine Ingredients for Pancake
instructions.text: Add the prepared seafood, green onions, and sliced yellow onion directly into the batter bowl. Gently mix until the seafood and vegetables are evenly coated with the batter.

instructions.title: Cook the Pancake
instructions.text: Heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a large non-stick pan or cast-iron skillet over medium-high heat. Once hot, pour half of the batter mixture into the pan, spreading it evenly to form a round pancake. Arrange some extra green onion pieces or red chili slices on top if desired.

instructions.title: Achieve Golden Crispiness
instructions.text: Cook for 5-7 minutes until the bottom is golden brown and crispy. Drizzle another tablespoon of oil around the edges, then carefully flip the pancake. Cook for another 5-7 minutes on the second side until it's also golden brown and cooked through. Repeat with the remaining batter and oil for a second pancake.

instructions.title: Serve Immediately
instructions.text: Transfer the cooked Haemul Pajeon to a cutting board and slice into wedges. Serve hot with the prepared dipping sauce. Enjoy the crispy texture and savory seafood flavors!

tips: For extra crispiness, ensure your pan is very hot before adding the batter and do not overcrowd the pan. Using a combination of all-purpose flour and rice flour also enhances crispiness.
Fresh, high-quality seafood is key to a delicious Haemul Pajeon. Feel free to customize with your favorite seafood like oysters, clams, or even octopus.
Serve immediately after cooking to enjoy the best crispy texture. Leftovers can be reheated in a dry pan for a few minutes to regain some crispness.
article:
Unlocking the secrets to truly delicious Korean cuisine at home often begins with mastering a few iconic dishes. Among them, Haemul Pajeon, the beloved Korean Seafood Pancake, stands out as a crispy, savory, and incredibly satisfying delight. Imagine a golden-brown pancake, generously studded with fresh seafood and vibrant green onions, offering a perfect harmony of textures and flavors. It's a popular street food, a comforting homemade meal, and a fantastic appetizer that perfectly complements any Korean spread.

This comprehensive guide will walk you through everything you need to know about making this exquisite dish, from understanding its origins to mastering the techniques for achieving that coveted crispy exterior. Get ready to impress your taste buds and your guests with an authentic Haemul Pajeon Recipe that’s both approachable and utterly delicious.

## What is Haemul Pajeon (Korean Seafood Pancake)?

Haemul Pajeon literally translates to "seafood green onion pancake." "Haemul" means seafood, "Pa" refers to green onions (scallions), and "Jeon" is a general term for pan-fried dishes made with a batter. This dish is far more than just a simple pancake; it's a culinary experience that balances the sweetness of fresh seafood, the pungent freshness of green onions, and a light, crispy batter.

Originating as a popular dish in coastal regions of Korea, Haemul Pajeon has evolved into a national favorite, enjoyed across the country in bustling markets, cozy restaurants, and family kitchens. Its appeal lies in its versatility and comforting nature. Unlike sweet breakfast pancakes, Haemul Pajeon is a savory dish, often served as an appetizer, a snack with Korean rice wine (Makgeolli), or even a light meal. The distinctive characteristic is the abundance of green onions, which are often laid whole or in long pieces across the pan, creating a beautiful visual and a burst of flavor in every bite.

## Ingredients You'll Need for Haemul Pajeon

Crafting the perfect Haemul Pajeon requires a thoughtful selection of fresh ingredients. Here’s a breakdown of what you’ll need for this fantastic Haemul Pajeon Recipe:

For the Pancake Batter:
*   1 cup all-purpose flour
*   1/4 cup rice flour (for extra crispiness)
*   1/2 teaspoon salt
*   1 large egg
*   1 1/4 cups very cold water (or ice water)

For the Filling:
*   2 cups green onions (scallions), cut into 2-3 inch pieces
*   1/2 cup mixed seafood (shrimp, squid, mussels, baby octopus – fresh or frozen, thawed and patted dry)
*   1/4 small onion, thinly sliced
*   1 red chili or bell pepper, thinly sliced (optional, for color and slight heat)
*   2-3 tablespoons vegetable oil (for frying)

For the Dipping Sauce:
*   2 tablespoons soy sauce
*   1 tablespoon rice vinegar
*   1 teaspoon sugar
*   1 teaspoon sesame oil
*   1 teaspoon toasted sesame seeds
*   1/2 teaspoon gochugaru (Korean chili flakes, optional)

The key to a truly exceptional Haemul Pajeon is the quality and freshness of your seafood and the sheer volume of green onions. Don't skimp on the green onions!

## Step-by-Step Instructions to Make Haemul Pajeon

Follow this detailed Haemul Pajeon Recipe to create a masterpiece in your own kitchen:

1.  **Prepare the Dipping Sauce:** In a small bowl, whisk together all the dipping sauce ingredients (soy sauce, rice vinegar, sugar, sesame oil, sesame seeds, and gochugaru if using). Set aside.
2.  **Prepare the Seafood and Vegetables:** If using frozen seafood, ensure it’s fully thawed and thoroughly patted dry with paper towels to prevent a watery batter. Cut larger shrimp or squid into bite-sized pieces. Wash and trim the green onions, then cut them into 2-3 inch lengths. Thinly slice the onion and red chili (if using).
3.  **Make the Batter:** In a large bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, rice flour, and salt. In a separate small bowl, whisk the egg and cold water. Gradually pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients, whisking until just combined. A few lumps are perfectly fine; overmixing will develop the gluten and make your pancake tough, not crispy.
4.  **Assemble and Cook the Pancake:** Heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a non-stick skillet (about 10-12 inches) over medium-high heat. Once the oil is shimmering, arrange half of your green onion pieces in a single layer across the bottom of the pan. Evenly scatter half of your prepared seafood, sliced onion, and red chili over the green onions.
5.  **Pour the Batter:** Carefully pour about half of the prepared batter over the seafood and vegetables, ensuring it evenly coats the ingredients and fills any gaps. Gently spread it out to form a round pancake.
6.  **Cook to Golden Perfection:** Cook for 3-5 minutes on the first side, or until the bottom is golden brown and crispy. You'll see the edges start to crisp up. Using a wide spatula, carefully flip the pancake. Drizzle another tablespoon of oil around the edges of the pan. Press down gently on the pancake with your spatula to ensure even contact with the pan and encourage crisping. Cook for another 3-5 minutes on the second side until it’s also golden brown and crispy, and the seafood is cooked through.
7.  **Serve:** Transfer the cooked Haemul Pajeon to a cutting board. Slice it into wedges or squares. Repeat the process for the second pancake (this recipe typically yields two medium-sized pancakes). Serve immediately with the prepared dipping sauce.

## Pro Tips for the Best Crispy Haemul Pajeon

Achieving that perfect crispy exterior with a tender, flavorful interior is the hallmark of a great Haemul Pajeon. Here are some professional tips to elevate your Haemul Pajeon Recipe:

*   **Ice Cold Water is Key:** Using very cold water or even ice water in your batter helps create a crispier pancake. The temperature difference between the cold batter and the hot oil creates steam, resulting in a lighter, crispier texture.
*   **Don't Overmix the Batter:** Lumps are your friend! Overmixing develops gluten, leading to a chewy, rather than crispy, pancake. Mix just enough until the dry ingredients are moistened.
*   **The Power of Rice Flour:** Incorporating rice flour (alongside all-purpose flour) is crucial for that extra crispness and a slightly chewy texture. Some even add a touch of cornstarch for an even lighter crunch.
*   **Generous Oil:** Don't be shy with the oil when frying. A good amount of hot oil is essential for achieving a beautifully golden and crispy crust.
*   **High Heat (Initially):** Start with medium-high heat to get a good sizzle and crisp up the exterior quickly. Once flipped, you can slightly reduce the heat to ensure the inside cooks through without burning the outside.
*   **Pat Seafood Dry:** Excess moisture from seafood can make your pancake soggy. Always pat thawed seafood thoroughly dry before adding it to the batter.
*   **Serve Immediately:** Haemul Pajeon is best enjoyed fresh off the pan while it’s still piping hot and at its crispiest.

## Serving Suggestions for Your Seafood Pancake

Haemul Pajeon is incredibly versatile and can be enjoyed in many ways. Serve your freshly made seafood pancake hot and sliced into wedges, accompanied by the tangy and savory dipping sauce.

*   **Appetizer or Snack:** It makes an excellent starter for a Korean meal or a delightful snack, especially when paired with Makgeolli (Korean rice wine) or Soju.
*   **Light Meal:** For a light lunch or dinner, a generous portion of Haemul Pajeon can be a satisfying meal on its own.
*   **With Banchan:** Serve it alongside other Korean side dishes (banchan) like kimchi, pickled radishes, or seasoned spinach for a more complete culinary experience.
*   **Game Day Treat:** It’s also a fantastic dish for entertaining, perfect for sharing with friends during a casual gathering or game night.

## Variations and Substitutions for Haemul Pajeon

While this Haemul Pajeon Recipe focuses on the classic seafood version, the beauty of jeon lies in its adaptability. Feel free to experiment with different fillings based on your preferences or what you have on hand:

*   **Vegetarian Pajeon (Yachaejeon):** Omit the seafood and load up on various vegetables. Sliced mushrooms (shiitake, oyster), zucchini, carrots, bell peppers, or even kimchi can make a delicious vegetarian pancake.
*   **Meat Pajeon (Gogi Jeon):** Add thinly sliced beef (marinated bulgogi-style) or pork for a hearty, savory alternative.
*   **Custom Seafood Mix:** Don't limit yourself to shrimp and squid. Oysters, clams, or other small, quick-cooking seafood varieties can be excellent additions. Just ensure they are fresh and prepared properly.
*   **Spicier Kick:** For those who love heat, add extra gochugaru to the batter or dipping sauce, or incorporate finely chopped fresh chilies (like Korean green chilies or jalapeños) into the pancake filling.
*   **Kimchi Pajeon (Kimchijeon):** A very popular variation where chopped kimchi is mixed into the batter and fried, often with pork or tuna.

Making Haemul Pajeon at home is a rewarding experience that brings a taste of authentic Korean comfort food right to your kitchen. With this detailed Haemul Pajeon Recipe and these helpful tips, you're well on your way to crafting a perfectly crispy, flavorful, and unforgettable seafood pancake. So gather your ingredients, fire up your skillet, and get ready to enjoy this delightful Korean classic!
